Register change_voice tool\n370\t        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n371\t            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n372\t\n373\t        voice.register_tool(\n374\t            name=\"change_voice\",\n375\t            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n376\t            handler=handle_voice_change,\n377\t            parameters={\n378\t                \"type\": \"object\",\n379\t                \"properties\": {\n380\t                    \"voice\": {\n381\t                        \"type\": \"string\",\n382\t                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n383\t                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n384\t                    }\n385\t                },\n386\t                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n387\t            }\n388\t        )\n389\t\n390\t        # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n437\t    if not utterance_id:\n438\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n439\t\n440\t    # Create utterance record\n441\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n442\t\n443\t    # Route the utterance\n444\t    try:\n445\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n446\t            utterance=utterance,\n447\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n448\t            session_id=session_id,\n449\t        )\n450\t\n451\t        # Process each routed intent\n452\t        intent_results = []\n453\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n454\t            # Create intent record in store\n455\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n456\t            await store.create_intent(\n457\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n458\t                session_id=session_id,\n459\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n460\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n461\t                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n462\t            )\n463\t\n464\t            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n465\t            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n466\t            intent_results.append(result)\n467\t\n468\t        return {\n469\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n470\t            \"intents\": intent_results,\n471\t        }\n472\t\n473\t    except Exception as e:\n474\t        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n475\t        return JSONResponse(\n476\t            status_code=500,\n477\t            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n478\t        )\n479\t\n480\t\n481\t@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\n482\tasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n483\t    \"\"\"\n484\t    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n485\t\n486\t    Phase 0 core query loop:\n487\t    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n488\t    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n489\t    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n490\t    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n491\t\n492\t    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n493\t    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n494\t    \"\"\"\n495\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n496\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n497\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n498\t    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n499\t\n500\t    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n501\t\n502\t    store = await get_store()\n503\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n504\t\n505\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n506\t    if not utterance_id:\n507\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n508\t\n509\t    # Create utterance record\n510\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n511\t\n512\t    # Route the utterance\n513\t    try:\n514\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n515\t            utterance=utterance,\n516\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n517\t            session_id=session_id,\n518\t        )\n519\t\n520\t        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n521\t        intent_tasks = []\n522\t        intent_ids = []\n523\t\n524\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n525\t            # Create intent record in store\n526\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n527\t            await store.create_intent(\n528\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n529\t                session_id=session_id,\n530\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n531\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n532\t                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n533\t            )\n534\t            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n535\t\n536\t            # Create task for parallel processing\n537\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n538\t                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n539\t                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n540\t            )\n541\t            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n542\t\n543\t        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n544\t        async def stream_results():\n545\t            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n546\t            results = []\n547\t\n548\t            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n549\t                try:\n550\t                    result = await task\n551\t                    results.append(result)\n552\t\n553\t                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n554\t                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n555\t                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n556\t                        # Include rendered card HTML in SSE so canvas injects it directly\n557\t                        # (Component card when matched, fallback when card_fallback=True)\n558\t                        sse_data = {\n559\t                            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n560\t                            \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n561\t                            \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n562\t                            \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n563\t                        }\n564\t                        # Add component_id when available (for canvas tracking)\n565\t                        if result.get(\"component_id\") is not None:\n566\t                            sse_data[\"component_id\"] = result[\"component_id\"]\n567\t                        # Add card_fallback flag when available (signals client to use fallback)\n568\t                        if result.get(\"card_fallback\") is not None:\n569\t                            sse_data[\"card_fallback\"] = result[\"card_fallback\"]\n570\t\n571\t                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n572\t                            SSEEvent(\n573\t                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n574\t                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n575\t                                data=sse_data,\n576\t                                rendered_html=result.get(\"rendered_html\"),\n577\t                            )\n578\t                        )\n579\t                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n580\t                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n581\t                        try:\n582\t                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n583\t                                intent_id,\n584\t                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n585\t                            )\n586\t                        except Exception as te:\n587\t                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n588\t\n589\t                except Exception as e:\n590\t                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n591\t                    results.append({\n592\t                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n593\t                        \"status\": \"error\",\n594\t                        \"error\": str(e),\n595\t                    })\n596\t\n597\t            return results\n598\t\n599\t        # Start parallel processing in background\n600\t        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n601\t\n602\t        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n603\t        return {\n604\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n605\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n606\t            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n607\t            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n608\t            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n609\t            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n610\t        }\n611\t\n612\t    except Exception as e:\n613\t        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n614\t        return JSONResponse(\n615\t            status_code=500,\n616\t            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n617\t        )\n618\t\n619\t\n620\t@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\n621\tasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n622\t    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n623\t\n624\t    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n625\t    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n626\t    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n627\t    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n628\t    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n629\t    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n630\t    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n631\t    stay NULL.\n632\t\n633\t    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n634\t    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n635\t    \"\"\"\n636\t    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n637\t    if not intent_id:\n638\t        return JSONResponse(\n639\t            status_code=400,\n640\t            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n641\t        )\n642\t\n643\t    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n644\t    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n645\t        value = request.get(name)\n646\t        if value is not None:\n647\t            fields[name] = int(value)\n648\t\n649\t    store = await get_store()\n650\t    try:\n651\t        # Upserts into the existing server-written row (created_at unchanged);\n652\t        # if no server row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n653\t        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n654\t        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n655\t    except Exception as e:\n656\t        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n657\t        return JSONResponse(\n658\t            status_code=500,\n659\t            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n660\t        )\n661\t\n662\t    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": Get API key from environment\n    api_key = os.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\")\n    if not api_key:\n        await websocket.send_json({\n            \"type\": \"error\",\n            \"error\": \"OpenAI API key not configured\"\n        })\n        await websocket.close(code=1011, reason=\"API key missing\")\n        return\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register audio surface\n    audio_surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"audio\")\n    surface_id = audio_surface_id\n    logger.info(f\"Registered audio surface: {surface_id}\")\n\n    # Load voice prompt\n    voice_prompt = load_voice_prompt(VOICE_PROMPT_PATH)\n\n    # Surface switch handler for audio-to-canvas continuity\n    async def on_surface_switch(surface_type: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle surface switch event (audio -> canvas).\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Surface switch: {surface_type}\")\n        try:\n            result = await handle_surface_switch(surface_type, session_id, voice)\n            # Send acknowledgment to client\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"adc.surface_switch_ack\",\n                \"surface\": surface_type,\n                \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n                \"summary\": result[\"catch_up_summary\"],\n            })\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Surface switch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n    # Memory extraction handler for turn completion\n    memory_handler = create_memory_handler(session_id=session_id, api_key=api_key)\n    if memory_handler:\n        logger.info(f\"Memory extraction enabled for session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Create voice session\n    voice = VoiceSession(\n        websocket=websocket,\n        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n        api_key=api_key,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        system_message=voice_prompt,\n        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n        logger=logger,\n        on_turn_done=memory_handler.on_turn_done if memory_handler else None,\n        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n    )\n\n    # Start result listener background task\n    listener_task = None\n    try:\n        listener_task = asyncio.create_task(\n            result_listener(session_id, voice, audio_surface_id)\n        )\n\n        # Register dispatch_intent tool\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"dispatch_intent\",\n            description=(\n                \"Dispatch a user utterance to the intent router. \"\n                \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n            ),\n            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"utterance\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Register change_voice tool\n        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"change_voice\",\n            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Run the session\n        await voice.run()\n\n    except WebSocketDisconnect:\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session disconnected: {session_id}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Voice session error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        try:\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e)\n            })\n        except:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        # Cancel listener task\n        if listener_task:\n            listener_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await listener_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Mark surface disconnected\n        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n\n\n@app.post(\"/router\")\nasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent router endpoint.\n\n    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n    Task-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads.\n    Other intents are routed to fetch + synthesize strands.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\nasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n\n    Phase 0 core query loop:\n    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n\n    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n        async def stream_results():\n            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n            results = []\n\n            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    results.append(result)\n\n                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n                        # Include rendered card HTML in SSE so canvas injects it directly\n                        # (Component card when matched, fallback when card_fallback=True)\n                        sse_data = {\n                            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                            \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n                            \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n                            \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n                        }\n                        # Add component_id when available (for canvas tracking)\n                        if result.get(\"component_id\") is not None:\n                            sse_data[\"component_id\"] = result[\"component_id\"]\n                        # Add card_fallback flag when available (signals client to use fallback)\n                        if result.get(\"card_fallback\") is not None:\n                            sse_data[\"card_fallback\"] = result[\"card_fallback\"]\n\n                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n                            SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                                data=sse_data,\n                                rendered_html=result.get(\"rendered_html\"),\n                            )\n                        )\n                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n                        try:\n                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n                                intent_id,\n                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n                            )\n                        except Exception as te:\n                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n                    results.append({\n                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                        \"status\": \"error\",\n                        \"error\": str(e),\n                    })\n\n            return results\n\n        # Start parallel processing in background\n        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n\n        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\nasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n\n    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n    stay NULL.\n\n    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n    \"\"\"\n    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n    if not intent_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n        )\n\n    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n        value = request.get(name)\n        if value is not None:\n            fields[name] = int(value)\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    try:\n        # Upserts into the existing server-written row (created_at unchanged);\n        # if no server row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n        )\n\n    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": list(fields.keys())}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\nasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per dispatch stage (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n\n    Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n    that has at least one captured sample. The latency-baseline bead consumes\n    the un-windowed store helper directly; this endpoint exposes the same\n    numbers over HTTP for the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist and on-demand\n    inspection. Optional ``since`` (unix timestamp) windows to recent\n    dispatches only.\n    \"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n    return await store.get_latency_percentiles(since=since)\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async handling.\n\n    For task-profile intents that require tracking and async execution:\n    - Formulates bead body via LLM\n    - Creates bead via bf CLI\n    - Returns pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    intent_type = request.get(\"intent_type\", \"task-profile\")\n    project_slug = request.get(\"project_slug\")\n    topic_id = request.get(\"topic_id\")\n    metadata = request.get(\"metadata\", {})\n    context = request.get(\"context\", {})\n\n    logger.info(f\"Escalating intent for session {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Create escalate request\n    escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n context=context,\n        metadata=metadata,\n    )\n\n    # Store the intent\n    store = await get_store()\n    if utterance_id:\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        escalate_request.intent_id = result.pending_card,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/environment\")\nasync def get_environment():\n    \"\"\"Return the discovered environment: all git repos and bead yet initialized\"}\n    entries = [\n        {\n            \"name\": e.name,\n            \"slug\": e.slug,\n            \"path\": str(e.path),\n            \"host\": e.host,\n            \"display_path\": e.display_path,\n            \"has_beads\": e.has_beads,\n            \"remote_url\": e.remote_url,\n            \"remote_name\": e.remote_name,\n        }\n        for e in the merged project registry (config/registry.yaml + discovery).\n\n    This is the DB-independent data source for the first-run welcome card (the\n    built-in family #2 — see plan, Component Library → Built-in cards, and Cold\n    start & demo seed).    \"local_repos\": s[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": s[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": s[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": s[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"scanned_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/events\")\nasync def canvas_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a content={\"error\": \"Topic manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n    return {\n        \"topics\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# API v1 Endpoints\n# =============================================================================\n\n# Pydantic models for API requests/responses\nclass SurfaceRegisterRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n\n\nclass HeartbeatRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n\n\nclass FeedbackRequestModel(BaseModel):\n    feedback: str\n    feedback_type: str = \"self_modification\"\n    context: Optional[dict] = None\n    session_id: Optional[str] = None\n    require_approval: bool = True\n\n\nclass ApprovalRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n\n\nclass BeadApproveRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n    session_id: str\n\n\nclass BeadRejectRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    reason: Optional[str] = None\n\n\nclass RollbackRequest(BaseModel):\n    artifact_name: str\n    artifact_type: str\n\n\nclass ComponentCreateRequest(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    description: str\n    html_template: str\n    change_note: str = \"Initial version\"\n\n\nclass ComponentUpdateRequest(BaseModel):\n    component_id: str\n    html_template: str\n    change_note: str\n\n\nclass ComponentIterateRequest(BaseModel):\n    component_id: str\n    feedback: str\n    result_data: Optional[dict] = None\n\n\nclass UsagePatternRecord(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"Request body for recording component usage patterns.\"\"\"\n    component_id: str\n    result_type: str\n    match_score: float\n    layout_bucket: str = \"normal\"\n\n\n# Canvas and Surface endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/register\")\nasync def api_v1_register_surface(request: SurfaceRegisterRequest):\n    \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    session = await store.get_session(request.session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n    else:\n        session_id = request.session_id\n\n    # Register surface\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(\n        session_id,\n        request.surface_type\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"session_id\": session_id\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat\")\nasync def {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics\")\nasync def api_v1_get_session_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"Get active topic cards for a session (canvas }\n\n\n@app.delete(\"/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/results/{result_id}\")\nasync def api_v1_delete_result(session_id: str, result_id: str):\n    \"\"\"Delete a result card by ID, ensuring it belongs to the specified session.\n\n    Used by the canvas to dismiss stuck/failed cards.\n    Returns the number of results deleted (0 or 1).\n    \"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Delete the result with session isolation\n    deletion_result = await store.delete_result(result_id, session_id)\n\n    return deletion_result\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/sse\")\nasync def api_v1_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: Optional[str] = None,\n    surface_type: str = \"canvas\"\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for topics, results, and component updates.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, surface_type)\n        logger.info(f\"Registered {surface_type} surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=surface_type,\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",\n        },\n    )\n\n\n# Feedback and Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def api_v1_approve_change(request: ApprovalRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve def api_v1_approve_bead(request: BeadApproveRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve a pending bead and create it via bf CLI.\n\n    Takes an approval_id from a pending_bead_approvals row, creates the bead,\n    and returns the pending card for the surface.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .escalate.handler import get_escalate_handler, EscalateRequest\n    import json\n\n    store = session_store_get_store()\n\n    # Get the pending approval\n    approval = await store.get_pending_approval(request.approval_id)\n    if not approval:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=404,\n            content={\"error\": \"Approval not found or expired\"}\n        )\n\n    if approval[\"status\"] != \"pending\":\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Approval already {approval['status']}\"}\n        )\n\n    if approval[\"session_id\"] != request.session_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=403,\n            content={\"error\": \"Session mismatch\"}\n        )\n\n    logger.info(f\"Approving bead {request.approval_id} for intent {approval['intent_id']}\")\n\n    try:\n        # Create the bead using the escalate handler\n        escalate_handler = get_escalate_handler(store)\n\n        # Build an escalate request from the approval data\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=approval[\"intent_id\"],\n            session_id=approval[\"session_id\"],\n            utterance=approval[\"utterance\"],\n            intent_type=\"action\",  # Default to action for approved beads\n            project_slug=approval[\"project_slug\"],\n            topic_id=approval[\"topic_id\"],\n        )\n\n        # Create the bead with the stored body\n        bead_id = await escalate_handler._create_bead_with_type(\n            request=escalate_request,\n            bead_body=approval[\"bead_body\"],\n            bead_type=approval[\"bead_type\"],\n        )\n\n        # Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n        await escalate_handler._create_bead_watch(\n            bead_ref=bead_id,\n            project_slug=approval[\"project_slug\"],\n            intent_type=\"action\",\n        )\n\n        # Update approval status\n        await store.update_approval_status(request.approval_id, \"approved\")\n\n        # Update intent status\n        await store.update_intent_status(\n            intent_id=approval[\"intent_id\"],\n            status=\"dispatched\",\n        )\n\n        # Build pending card\n        pending_card = escalate_handler.build_pending_card(\n            request=escalate_request,\n            bead_id=bead_id,\n            bead_type=approval[\"bead_type\"],\n        )\n\n        # Broadcast result_created event to canvas\n        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n            SSEEvent(\n                event_type=EventType.RESULT_CREATED,\n                data={\n                    \"intent_id\": approval[\"intent_id\"],\n                    \"session_id\": approval[\"session_id\"],\n                    \"result_id\": f\"result-{bead_id}\",  # Use bead_id as result_id\n                    \"topic_id\": approval[\"topic_id\"],\n                    \"summary\": pending_card[\"summary\"],\n                    \"urgency\": pending_card[\"urgency\"],\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                },\n                target_session_id=approval[\"session_id\"],\n            )\n        )\n\n        logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} from approval {request.approval_id}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"approved\",\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"pending_card\": pending_card,\n            \"message\": f\"Bead {bead_id} created successfully\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to approve bead {request.approval_id}: {e}\")\n        # Update approval status to rejected on error\n        await store.update_approval_status(request.approval_id, \"rejected\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to create bead: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/beads/reject\")\nasync def api_v1_reject_bead(request: BeadRejectRequest):\n    \"\"\"Reject a pending bead approval.\n\n    Marks the approval as rejected and updates the intent status.\n    \"\"\"\n    store = session_store_get_store()\n\n    # Get the pending approval\n    approval = await store.get_pending_approval(request.approval_id)\n    if not approval:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=404,\n            content={\"error\": \"Approval not found or expired\"}\n        )\n\n    if approval[\"session_id\"] != request.session_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=403,\n            content={\"error\": \"Session mismatch\"}\n        )\n\n    logger.info(f\"Rejecting bead approval {request.approval_id}\")\n\n    # Update approval status\n    await store.update_approval_status(request.approval_id, \"rejected\")\n\n    # Update intent status to cancelled\n    await SSEEvent(\n            event_type=EventType.INTENT_RESOLVED,\n            data={\n                \"intent_id\": approval[\"intent_id\"],\n                \"session_id\": approval[\"session_id\"],\n                \"status\": \"cancelled\",\n                \"message\": \"Bead approval was rejected\",\n                \"reason\": request.reason,\n            },\n            target_session_id=approval[\"session_id\"],\n        )\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": \"rejected\",\n        \"message\": \"Bead approval rejected\",\n    }\n\n\n# Component Library =============================================================================\n# Usage patterns routes (must come before /{component_id} routes to avoid conflicts)\n# =============================================================================\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/patterns\")\nasync def api_v1_record_pattern(request: UsagePatternRecord):\n    \"\"\"Record or update a component usage pattern.\n\n    Simple endpoint for recording component usage patterns.\n    Upserts pattern (update if exists, insert if new) and sets updated_at to current timestamp.\n\n    Request body:\n        - result_type: str - The type of result\n        - component_id: str - The component that was used\n        - layout_bucket: str - The layout bucket used (default 'normal')\n        - match_score: float - How well the component matched (0.0-1.0)\n\n    Returns:\n        {\"status\": \"ok\", \"pattern\": {...}} on success\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    try:\n        # Validate inputs\n        if not request.component_id or not request.result_type:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": \"component_id and result_type are required\"}\n            )\n\n        if not 0.0 <= request.match_score <= 1.0:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": \"match_score must be between 0.0 and 1.0\"}\n            )\n\n        if request.layout_bucket not in _component_library.LAYOUT_BUCKETS:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": f\"layout_bucket must be one of {', '.join(_component_library.LAYOUT_BUCKETS)}\"}\n            )\n\n        # Record the pattern (upsert: update if exists, insert if new)\n        _component_library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=request.component_id,\n            result_type=request.result_type,\n            match_score=request.match_score,\n            layout_bucket=request.layout_bucket,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Recorded usage pattern: component={request.component_id}, \"\n            f\"result_type={request.result_type}, layout_bucket={request.layout_bucket}, \"\n            f\"match_score={request.match_score}\"\n        )\n\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"ok\",\n            \"pattern\": {\n                \"component_id\": request.component_id,\n                \"result_type\": request.result_type,\n                \"layout_bucket\": request.layout_bucket,\n                \"match_score\": request.match_score,\n            }\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to record usage pattern: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record usage pattern: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/patterns\")\nasync def api_v1_get_patterns(result_type: str = Query(..., description=\"Result type to filter patterns (required)\")):\n    \"\"\"Get component usage patterns by result_type.\n\n    Query params:\n        result_type: Filter by result type (required)\n\n    Returns:\n        {\"patterns\": [...]} ordered by match_score DESC\n        404 if no patterns found for the result_type\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    try:\n        import sqlite3\n\n        conn = _component_library._get_conn()\n\n        # Query patterns for the specified result_type, ordered by match_score DESC\n        query = \"\"\"\n            SELECT result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count, updated_at\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n        \"\"\"\n\n        rows = conn.execute(query, (result_type,)).fetchall()\n\n        # Return 404 if no patterns found\n        if not rows:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=404,\n                content={\"error\": f\"No patterns found for result_type: {result_type}\"}\n            )\n\n        patterns = [\n            {\n                \"result_type\": row[0],\n                \"component_id\": row[1],\n                \"layout_bucket\": row[2],\n                \"match_score\": row[3],\n                \"sample_count\": row[4],\n                \"updated_at\": row[5],\n            }\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n        return {\n            \"patterns\": patterns,\n            \"count\": len(patterns),\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to get usage patterns: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to get usage patterns: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/components/usage-patterns\")\nasync def api_v1_record_usage_pattern(request: UsagePatternRecord):\n    \"\"\"Record or update a component Validate inputs\n        if not request.component_id or not request.result_type:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": \"component_id api_v1_list_usage_patterns(\n    result_type: Optional[str] = Query(None, description=\"Filter by result type\"),\n    component_id: Optional[str] = Query(None, description=\"Filter by component ID\"),\n    limit: int = Query(100, description=\"Maximum number of patterns to return\"),\n):\n    \"\"\"List component usage patterns, optionally filtered.\n\n    Query params:\n        result_type: Filter by result type (optional)\n        component_id: Filter by component ID (optional)\n        limit: Maximum number of patterns to return (default 100)\n\n    Returns:\n        {\"patterns\": [...], \"count\": <total>}\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    try:\n        import sqlite3\n\n        conn = _component_library._get_conn()\n\n        # Build query with filters\n        where_clauses = []\n        params = []\n\n        if result_type:\n            where_clauses.append(\"result_type = ?\")\n            params.append(result_type)\n\n        if component_id:\n            where_clauses.append(\"component_id = ?\")\n            params.append(component_id)\n\n        where_sql = \" AND \".join(where_clauses) if where_clauses else \"1=1\"\n\n        query = f\"\"\"\n            SELECT result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count, updated_at\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE {where_sql}\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT ?\n        \"\"\"\n\n        params.append(limit)\n\n        rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\n\n        patterns = [\n            {\n                \"result_type\": row[0],\n                \"component_id\": row[1],\n                \"layout_bucket\": row[2],\n                \"match_score\": row[3],\n                \"sample_count\": row[4],\n                \"updated_at\": row[5],\n            }\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n        # Get total count\n        count_query = f\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM component_usage_patterns WHERE {where_sql}\"\n        count_params = params[:-1]  # Exclude limit\n        total = conn.execute(count_query, count_params).fetchone()[0]\n\n        return {\n            \"patterns\": patterns,\n            \"count\": total,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to list usage patterns: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to list usage patterns: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# git_show(ref: str, cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> GitResult:\n151\t    \"\"\"\n152\t    Show git object content (e.g., 'HEAD:path/to/file').\n153\t\n154\t    Args:\n155\t        ref: Git reference (e.g., 'HEAD:path/to/file')\n156\t        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n157\t\n158\t    Returns:\n159\t        GitResult with show output\n160\t    \"\"\"\n161\t    return run_git_command(['show', ref], cwd=cwd)\n162\t\n163\t\n164\tdef git_rev_parse(ref: str, short: bool = False, cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> GitResult:\n165\t    \"\"\"\n166\t    Get git SHA for a reference.\n167\t\n168\t    Args:\n169\t        ref: Git reference (e.g., 'HEAD')\n170\t        short: If True, return short SHA (default: False)\n171\t        cwd: Working directory (defaults to args.append('--short')\n179\t    args.append(ref)\n180\t    return run_git_command(args, cwd=cwd)\n181\t\n182\t\n183\tdef generate_self_mod_commit_message(file_path: Path, cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> str:\n184\t    \"\"\"\n185\t    Generate a standardized commit message for self-modification writes.\n186\t\n187\t    Creates a commit message with the format:\n188\t    'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n189\t    where <commit-short-sha> is the short SHA of the current HEAD (the commit\n190\t    we are building on top of).\n191\t\n192\t    Args:\n193\t        file_path: Path to the file being modified (relative or absolute)\n194\t        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n195\t\n196\t    Returns:\n197\t        Commit message string with path and optional previous commit SHA\n198\t    \"\"\"\n199\t    if cwd is None:\n200\t        cwd = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n201\t\n202\t    # Get relative path from repo root\n203\t    try:\n204\t        rel_path = Path(file_path).relative_to(cwd)\n205\t    except ValueError:\n206\t        # If file_path is already relative or outside repo, use as-is\n207\t        rel_path = Path(file_path)\n208\t\n209\t    # Get the short SHA of the current HEAD (previous commit)\n210\t    # This will be included in the commit message to show what we're building on\n211\t    head_result = git_rev_parse('HEAD', short=True, cwd=cwd)\n212\t\n213\t    if head_result.success and head_result.stdout.strip():\n214\t        prev_commit_sha = head_result.stdout.strip()\n215\t        return f\"auto: self-mod write to {rel_path} [{prev_commit_sha}]\"\n216\t    else:\n217\t        # No previous commit (e.g., initial commit or empty repo)\n218\t        return f\"auto: self-mod write to {rel_path}\"\n219\t\n220\t# Prompt paths read per-invocation so edits take effect without a server restart\n221\t# (hot-reload), matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py and\n222\t# src/intent/router.py.\n223\tSELF_MOD_PARSE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/self_mod_parse.md\")\n224\tSELF_MOD_GENERATE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/self_mod_generate.md\")\n225\t\n226\t# Fallbacks used only if a prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n227\t_PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n228\t    \"You classify a user instruction to the artifact it targets. \"\n229\t    'Return ONLY JSON: {\"artifact_type\": \"prompt|config|component\", '\n230\t    '\"artifact_name\": \"<name>\", \"reasoning\": \"...\"}.'\n231\t)\n232\t_GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n233\t    \"You apply a user instruction to an artifact. Return ONLY JSON: \"\n234\t    '{\"updated_content\": \"<full updated text>\", \"change_summary\": \"one sentence\"}.'\n235\t)\n236\t\n237\t\n238\tclass ArtifactType(Enum):\n239\t    \"\"\"Types of artifacts that can be modified.\"\"\"\n240\t    PROMPT = \"prompt\"\n241\t    CONFIG = \"config\"\n242\t    COMPONENT = \"component\"\n243\t\n244\t\n245\t@dataclass\n246\tclass ArtifactDiff:\n247\t    \"\"\"A diff showing changes to an artifact.\"\"\"\n248\t    artifact_name: str\n249\t    artifact_type: ArtifactType\n250\t    before: str\n251\t    after: str\n252\t    change_summary: str\n253\t    confidence: float\n254\t\n255\t\n256\t@dataclass\n257\tclass ModificationRequest:\n258\t    \"\"\"A user request to modify system behavior.\"\"\"\n259\t    instruction: str\n260\t    artifact_name: Optional[str]\n261\t    artifact_type: Optional[ArtifactType]\n262\t    context: Dict[str, Any]\n263\t\n264\t\n265\tclass SelfModificationAgent:\n266\t    \"\"\"\n267\t    Agent that modifies system artifacts based on user feedback.\n268\t\n269\t    Workflow:\n270\t    1. Receive user instruction\n271\t    2. Identify target artifact\n272\t    3. Read current artifact content\n273\t    4. Generate update\n274\t    5. Surface diff to user\n275\t    6. On approval: write artifact\n276\t    7. On rejection: discard\n277\t    \"\"\"\n278\t\n279\t    def __init__(\n280\t        self,\n281\t        parse_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n282\t        generate_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n283\t    ):\n284\t        self.reload_mgr = get_reload_manager()\n285\t        self.component_library = get_library()\n286\t        self._pending_diffs: List[ArtifactDiff] = []\n287\t        self.parse_prompt_path = parse_prompt_path or SELF_MOD_PARSE_PROMPT_PATH\n288\t        self.generate_prompt_path = generate_prompt_path or SELF_MOD_GENERATE_PROMPT_PATH\n289\t        self._zai_client = None\n290\t\n291\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n292\t        \"\"\"Get or create the ZAI proxy client (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n293\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n294\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n295\t        return self._zai_client\n296\t\n297\t    def _load_prompt(self, path: Path, fallback: str) -> str:\n298\t        \"\"\"Load a self-modification prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n299\t        try:\n300\t            return path.read_text()\n301\t        except Exception as e:\n302\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load prompt {path}: {e}\")\n303\t            return fallback\n304\t\n305\t    def _available_artifacts(self) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n306\t        \"\"\"Build the list of registered artifacts for the parser prompt.\"\"\"\n307\t        artifacts: List[Dict[str, str]] = []\n308\t        for name, path_str in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().items():\n309\t            suffix = Path(path_str).suffix.lower()\n310\t            type_str = \"config\" if suffix in (\".yaml\", \".yml\") else \"prompt\"\n311\t            artifacts.append({\"name\": name, \"type\": type_str})\n312\t        return artifacts\n313\t\n314\t    @staticmethod\n315\t    def _strip_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n316\t        \"\"\"Strip ```json ... ``` markdown fences from a GLM response.\"\"\"\n317\t        raw = raw.strip()\n318\t        if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n319\t            raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n320\t            raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n321\t        return raw\n322\t\n323\t    async def process_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ArtifactDiff:\n324\t        \"\"\"\n325\t        Process a user instruction for system modification.\n326\t\n327\t        Args:\n328\t            instruction: Natural language instruction\n329\t\n330\t        Returns:\n331\t            The proposed diff for user approval\n332\t        \"\"\"\n333\t        # Parse the instruction to identify target (LLM call)\n334\t        request = await self._parse_instruction(instruction)\n335\t\n336\t        # Get current content\n337\t        current_content = self._get_artifact_content(request)\n338\t\n339\t        # Generate update (LLM call)\n340\t        updated_content, change_summary = await self._generate_update(\n341\t            request,\n342\t            current_content\n343\t        )\n344\t\n345\t        diff = ArtifactDiff(\n346\t            artifact_name=request.artifact_name or \"unknown\",\n347\t            artifact_type=request.artifact_type or ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n348\t            before=current_content,\n349\t            after=updated_content,\n350\t            change_summary=change_summary,\n351\t            confidence=self._estimate_confidence(request, change_summary)\n352\t        )\n353\t\n354\t        self._pending_diffs.append(diff)\n355\t        return diff\n356\t\n357\t    async def _parse_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ModificationRequest:\n358\t        \"\"\"\n359\t        Parse an instruction to identify the target artifact via an LLM call.\n360\t\n361\t        The LLM classifies the free-text instruction against the registered\n362\t        artifacts and returns the artifact_type + artifact_name. Falls back to\n363\t        the router prompt if the call or its response cannot be parsed.\n364\t        \"\"\"\n365\t        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(self.parse_prompt_path, _PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK)\n366\t        user_message = (\n367\t            \"## Registered Artifacts\\n\"\n368\t            + json.dumps(self._available_artifacts(), indent=2)\n369\t            + f\"\\n\\n## User Instruction\\n{instruction}\\n\"\n370\t        )\n371\t\n372\t        try:\n373\t            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n374\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n375\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n376\t                user_message=user_message,\n377\t                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # cheap, fast classification\n378\t                max_tokens=512,\n379\t                temperature=0.0,  # deterministic classification\n380\t            )\n381\t            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n382\t\n383\t            type_str = data.get(\"artifact_type\", \"prompt\")\n384\t            name = data.get(\"artifact_name\")\n385\t\n386\t            try:\n387\t                artifact_type = ArtifactType(type_str)\n388\t            except ValueError:\n389\t                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n390\t\n391\t            # Validate the name is actually registered; fall back to a known\n392\t            # artifact (preferring the router prompt) so we never target a\n393\t            # non-existent artifact.\n394\t            registered = list(self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().keys())\n395\t            registered_set = set(registered)\n396\t            if artifact_type != ArtifactType.COMPONENT and (\n397\t                not name or name not in registered_set\n398\t            ):\n399\t                if \"router\" in registered_set:\n400\t                    name = \"router\"\n401\t                elif registered:\n402\t                    name = registered[0]\n403\t                else:\n404\t                    name = \"unknown\"\n405\t                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n406\t\n407\t            return ModificationRequest(\n408\t                instruction=instruction,\n409\t                artifact_name=name,\n410\t                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n411\t                context={\n412\t                    \"raw_instruction\": instruction,\n413\t                    \"reasoning\": data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n414\t                },\n415\t            )\n416\t        except Exception as e:\n417\t            logger.warning(\n418\t                f\"_parse_instruction LLM parse failed, defaulting to router prompt: {e}\"\n419\t            )\n420\t            return ModificationRequest(\n421\t                instruction=instruction,\n422\t                artifact_name=\"router\",\n423\t                artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n424\t                context={\"raw_instruction\": instruction, \"fallback\": True},\n425\t            )\n426\t\n427\t    def _get_artifact_content(self, request: ModificationRequest) -> str:\n428\t        \"\"\"Get current content of the target artifact.\"\"\"\n429\t        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n430\t            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n431\t                return self.reload_mgr.get_prompt(request.artifact_name)\n432\t        elif request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n433\t            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n434\t                # For configs, return YAML as string for diff\n435\t                artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(request.artifact_name)\n436\t                if artifact:\n437\t                    return artifact.content\n438\t\n439\t        return \"# Artifact not found or not loaded\"\n440\t\n441\t    async def _generate_update(\n442\t        self,\n443\t        request: ModificationRequest,\n444\t        current_content: str\n445\t    ) -> Tuple[str, str]:\n446\t        \"\"\"\n447\t        Generate updated artifact content via an LLM call.\n448\t\n449\t        Sends the current content + instruction to the LLM and returns the full\n450\t        updated content plus a change summary. On failure, returns the content\n451\t        unchanged with an honest summary rather than fabricating a change.\n452\t        \"\"\"\n453\t        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(\n454\t            self.generate_prompt_path, _GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n455\t        )\n456\t        artifact_type = request.artifact_type.value if request.artifact_type else \"prompt\"\n457\t        user_message = (\n458\t            f\"## Instruction\\n{request.instruction}\\n\\n\"\n459\t            f\"## Artifact Type\\n{artifact_type}\\n\\n\"\n460\t            f\"## Current Content\\n```\\n{current_content}\\n```\\n\"\n461\t        )\n462\t\n463\t        try:\n464\t            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n465\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n466\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n467\t                user_message=user_message,\n468\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,  # higher quality for rewriting\n469\t                max_tokens=4096,\n470\t                temperature=0.2,\n471\t            )\n472\t            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n473\t\n474\t            updated = data.get(\"updated_content\")\n475\t            summary = data.get(\"change_summary\", \"\")\n476\t            if not isinstance(updated, str) or not updated.strip():\n477\t                logger.warning(\n478\t                    \"_generate_update returned no updated_content; leaving artifact unchanged\"\n479\t                )\n480\t                return current_content, summary or \"No update generated\"\n481\t            return updated, summary or \"Updated artifact\"\n482\t        except Exception as e:\n483\t            logger.error(f\"_generate_update LLM call failed: {e}\")\n484\t            return current_content, f\"Update generation failed: {e}\"\n485\t\n486\t    def _estimate_confidence(\n487\t        self,\n488\t        request: ModificationRequest,\n489\t        change_summary: str\n490\t    ) -> float:\n491\t        \"\"\"\n492\t        Estimate confidence in the proposed change.\n493\t\n494\t        Higher confidence for:\n495\t        - Clear, specific instructions\n496\t        - Additive changes (vs destructive)\n497\t        - Low-risk artifacts (prompts vs registry)\n498\t        \"\"\"\n499\t        confidence = 0.5  # Base confidence\n500\t\n501\t        instruction_lower = request.instruction.lower()\n502\t\n503\t        # Specific instructions increase confidence\n504\t        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"add\", \"include\", \"always\"]):\n505\t            confidence += 0.2\n506\t\n507\t        # Destructive keywords decrease confidence\n508\t        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"remove\", \"delete\", \"change entirely\"]):\n509\t            confidence -= 0.2\n510\t\n511\t        # Config changes are riskier than prompt changes\n512\t        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n513\t            confidence -= 0.1\n514\t\n515\t        # Clamp to valid range\n516\t        return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))\n517\t\n518\t    def apply_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n519\t        \"\"\"\n520\t        Apply a diff by writing the updated artifact.\n521\t\n522\t        Args:\n523\t            diff: The diff to apply\n524\t\n525\t        Returns:\n526\t            True if successful, False otherwise\n527\t        \"\"\"\n528\t        try:\n529\t            if diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n530\t                return self._write_prompt(diff)\n531\t            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n532\t                return self._write_config(diff)\n533\t            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n534\t                return self._write_component(diff)\n535\t            return False\n536\t        except Exception as e:\n537\t            print(f\"Failed to apply diff: {e}\")\n538\t            return False\n539\t\n540\t    def _commit_artifact_write(self, artifact_path: Path, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> None:\n541\t        \"\"\"\n542\t        Create a git commit for an artifact write.\n543\t\n544\t        Creates a commit with a machine-generated message following the convention:\n545\t        'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n546\t\n547\t        Args:\n548\t            artifact_path: Path to the artifact that was written\n549\t            artifact_type: Type of artifact (prompt/config)\n550\t        \"\"\"\n551\t        try:\n552\t            # Get the repo root directory\n553\t            repo_root = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n554\t\n555\t            # Get relative path from repo root\n556\t            rel_path = artifact_path.relative_to(repo_root)\n557\t\n558\t            # Verify the file exists before trying to stage it\n559\t            if not artifact_path.exists():\n560\t                logger.error(f\"Cannot commit artifact write: file does not exist at {artifact_path}\")\n561\t                return\n562\t\n563\t            # Stage the file for commit\n564\t            # This works for new files, modified files, and files that were deleted then recreated\n565\t            add_result = subprocess.run(\n566\t                ['git', 'add', str(rel_path)],\n567\t                cwd=repo_root,\n568\t                capture_output=True,\n569\t                text=True,\n570\t                check=False,\n571\t                timeout=10\n572\t            )\n573\t\n574\t            if add_result.returncode != 0:\n575\t                logger.error(f\"git add failed: stderr={add_result.stderr}, stdout={add_result.stdout}, returncode={add_result.returncode}\")\n576\t                return\n577\t\n578\t            # Generate standardized commit message with previous commit SHA\n579\t            commit_msg = generate_self_mod_commit_message(rel_path, cwd=repo_root)\n580\t\n581\t            # Create the commit (file is now staged)\n582\t            # Only commit staged changes, ignoring other unstaged changes\n583\t            result = subprocess.run(\n584\t                ['git', 'commit', '-m', commit_msg],\n585\t                cwd=repo_root,\n586\t                capture_output=True,\n587\t                text=True,\n588\t                check=False,\n589\t                timeout=10\n590\t            )\n591\t\n592\t            # Only log if commit was successful\n593\t            if result.returncode == 0:\n594\t                # Get the short SHA of the commit just created\n595\t                sha_result = subprocess.run(\n596\t                    ['git', 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD'],\n597\t                    cwd=repo_root,\n598\t                    capture_output=True,\n599\t                    text=True,\n600\t                    check=True,\n601\t                    timeout=10\n602\t                )\n603\t\n604\t                short_sha = sha_result.stdout.strip()\n605\t                logger.info(f\"Created git commit {short_sha} for {artifact_type.value} write to {rel_path}\")\n606\t            else:\n607\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to create git commit: stdout={result.stdout}, stderr={result.stderr}, returncode={result.returncode}\")\n608\t\n609\t        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n610\t            logger.error(\"Git command timed out\")\n611\t        except Exception as e:\n612\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create git commit for artifact write: {e}\")\n613\t\n614\t    def _write_prompt(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n615\t        \"\"\"Write updated prompt file.\"\"\"\n616\t        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n617\t        if not artifact:\n618\t            return False\n619\t\n620\t        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n621\t            f.write(diff.after)\n622\t\n623\t        # Force reload to pick up changes\n624\t        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n625\t\n626\t        # Create git commit for the prompt write\n627\t        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n628\t\n629\t        return True\n630\t\n631\t    def _write_config(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n632\t        \"\"\"Write updated config file.\"\"\"\n633\t        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n634\t        if not artifact:\n635\t            return False\n636\t\n637\t        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n638\t            f.write(diff.after)\n639\t\n640\t        # Force reload\n641\t        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n642\t\n643\t        # Create git commit for the config write\n644\t        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n645\t\n646\t        return True\n647\t\n648\t    def _write_component(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n649\t        \"\"\"Write updated component to library.\"\"\"\n650\t        # For components, we need to identify the component\n651\t        # This is a placeholder - in production, parse component_id from instruction\n652\t        if not diff.artifact_name.startswith(\"comp-\"):\n653\t            return False\n654\t\n655\t        component = self.component_library.get_component(diff.artifact_name)\n656\t        if not component:\n657\t            return False\n658\t\n659\t        self.component_library.update_component(\n660\t            component.id,\n661\t            diff.after,\n662\t            diff.change_summary\n663\t        )\n664\t        return True\n665\t\n666\t    def reject_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff):\n667\t        \"\"\"Discard a diff without applying it.\"\"\"\n668\t        if diff in self._pending_diffs:\n669\t            self._pending_diffs.remove(diff)\n670\t\n671\t    def rollback(self, artifact_name: str, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> bool:\n672\t        \"\"\"\n673\t        Rollback an artifact to its previous version.\n674\t\n675\t        For prompts/configs: read from git history\n676\t        For components: use component version history\n677\t        \"\"\"\n678\t        if artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n679\t            return self._rollback_component(artifact_name)\n680\t\n681\t        # For prompts/configs, use git to get previous version\n682\t        import subprocess\n683\t        try:\n684\t            artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(artifact_name)\n685\t            if not artifact:\n686\t                return False\n687\t\n688\t            # Get previous version from git\n689\t            result = subprocess.run(\n690\t                ['git', 'show', f'HEAD:{artifact.path.name}'],\n691\t                capture_output=True,\n692\t                text=True,\n693\t                cwd=artifact.path.parent\n694\t            )\n695\t\n696\t            if result.returncode == 0:\n697\t                with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n698\t                    f.write(result.stdout)\n699\t                self.reload_mgr.force_reload(artifact_name)\n700\t                return True\n701\t        except Exception as e:\n702\t            print(f\"Rollback failed: {e}\")\n703\t\n704\t        return False\n705\t\n706\t    def _rollback_component(self, component_id: str) -> bool:\n707\t        \"\"\"Rollback a component using its version history.\"\"\"\n708\t        component = self.component_library.get_component(component_id)\n709\t        if not component or component.version <= 1:\n710\t            return False\n711\t\n712\t        target_version = component.version - 1\n713\t        self.component_library.rollback_component(component_id, target_version)\n714\t        return True\n715\t\n716\t    def list_pending_diffs(self) -> List[ArtifactDiff]:\n717\t        \"\"\"Get all pending diffs awaiting approval.\"\"\"\n718\t        return self.reload_mgr = get_reload_manager()\n        self.component_library = get_library()\n        self._pending_diffs: List[ArtifactDiff] = []\n        self.parse_prompt_path = parse_prompt_path or
